lede: A look at April 8 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including A.J. & Big Justice and Yemane Tsegay, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 8 April 2026 Israeli attacks on Lebanon
events.desc: Operation Eternal Darkness: Hours after a ceasefire to the Iran war, Israeli attacks on Lebanon kill at least 357 and injures more than 1200 people in the largest airstrikes of the Lebanon war.

events.title: Barack Obama
events.desc: U.S. President Barack Obama and Russian President Dmitry Medvedev sign the New START Treaty.

events.title: Voyageurs National Park
events.desc: Voyageurs National Park is established by the U.S. Congress.

events.title: BOAC Flight 712
events.desc: BOAC Flight 712 catches fire shortly after takeoff. As a result of her actions in the accident, Barbara Jane Harrison is awarded a posthumous George Cross, the only GC awarded to a woman in peacetime.

events.title: Organization of American States
events.desc: The Organization of American States drafts an agreement to create the Inter-American Development Bank.

events.title: Western Australia
events.desc: The Australian state of Western Australia votes to secede from the federation in a non-binding referendum, although efforts to implement the result prove unsuccessful.

events.title: French Third Republic
events.desc: The French Third Republic and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland sign the Entente cordiale.

events.title: Venus de Milo
events.desc: The Venus de Milo is discovered on the Aegean island of Milos.

events.title: Alexander I of Russia
events.desc: Czar Alexander I, the Russian Emperor and the Grand Duke of Finland, officially announces the transfer of the status of the Finnish capital from Turku to Helsinki.

events.title: Mongol conquest of the Jin dynasty
events.desc: Mongol–Jin War: The Mongols begin their siege on Kaifeng, the capital of the Jin dynasty.

births.name: Barbara Young, Baroness Young of Old Scone
Julius Seljamaa
births.line: Estonian journalist and politician, Minister of Foreign Affairs of Estonia (died 1936)

deaths.text: Nelsy Cruz, Dominican politician, governor of Monte Cristi
Margaret Thatcher, English politician, first female Prime Minister of the United Kingdom
Henry Sydney, 1st Earl of Romney, English colonel and politician, Lord Lieutenant of Ireland

observances.text: Buddha's Birthday, also known as Hana Matsuri, "Flower Festival" (Japan)
Christian feast day: Pope Dionysius of Alexandria
Christian feast day: Julie Billiart of Namur
